bullets.text: The US has lifted its naval blockade on Iran, with Iran's supreme leader stating that Donald Trump agreed to the deal "out of desperation." BBC analysts are assessing what both Washington and Tehran gain from the deal and the challenges they face in maintaining it.

Residents in Moscow have reported black rain after nearly 200 Ukrainian drones struck an oil refinery and a shopping center southeast of the Russian capital, marking one of the largest Ukrainian attacks.

The CDC is allocating $107 million in emergency funding to combat the Ebola response in the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, as the number of infected individuals has surpassed 1,000, although health officials maintain the global risk remains low.

An auditor-general's report indicates that fire authorities failed to meet planned fuel reduction targets ahead of two disastrous fire seasons, warning that properties could be at risk if strategic fuel breaks are not implemented.

The Albanese government is facing policy challenges, particularly with announced changes to its tax package, which aim to address a significant post-budget backlash from various interest groups and commentators.

Pauline Hanson, leader of the right-wing party One Nation, used her first address to the National Press Club to criticize the press and discussed party policy on flying only the Australian flag with a new Farrer MP.

The US-Iran accord may lead to new charges for shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, with industry executives concerned about potential fees after 60 days or a fund similar to that in the Strait of Malacca.
Intel's stock surged after President Trump announced that the chipmaker would collaborate with Apple Inc. to design and produce semiconductors domestically, boosting market confidence in the company.
A record surge in Treasury futures trading reflects increased bets on a Federal Reserve interest rate hike in July, indicating growing market expectations for tighter monetary policy.
